record_types.name: INT_EP_CTRL_Register

fields.name: Reserved_0_0
fields.description: Host: Enable interrupt endpoint 1 -> 15

fields.name: INT_EP_ACTIVE
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: INTE_Register

fields.name: HOST_CONN_DIS
fields.description:
Host: raised when a device wakes up the host. Cleared by writing to
SIE_STATUS.RESUME

fields.name: HOST_RESUME
fields.description:
Host: raised every time the host sends a SOF (Start of Frame).
Cleared by reading SOF_RD

fields.name: HOST_SOF
fields.description:
Raised every time SIE_STATUS.TRANS_COMPLETE is set. Clear by writing
to this bit.

fields.name: TRANS_COMPLETE
fields.description:
Raised when any bit in BUFF_STATUS is set. Clear by clearing all bits
in BUFF_STATUS.

fields.name: BUFF_STATUS
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.DATA_SEQ_ERROR

fields.name: ERROR_DATA_SEQ
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.RX_TIMEOUT

fields.name: ERROR_RX_TIMEOUT
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.RX_OVERFLOW

fields.name: ERROR_RX_OVERFLOW
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.BIT_STUFF_ERROR

fields.name: ERROR_BIT_STUFF
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.CRC_ERROR

fields.name: ERROR_CRC
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.STALL_REC

fields.name: STALL
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.VBUS_DETECTED

fields.name: VBUS_DETECT
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.BUS_RESET

fields.name: BUS_RESET
fields.description:
Set when the device connection state changes. Cleared by writing to
SIE_STATUS.CONNECTED

fields.name: DEV_CONN_DIS
fields.description:
Set when the device suspend state changes. Cleared by writing to
SIE_STATUS.SUSPENDED

fields.name: DEV_SUSPEND
fields.description:
Set when the device receives a resume from the host. Cleared by
writing to SIE_STATUS.RESUME

fields.name: DEV_RESUME_FROM_HOST
fields.description: Device. Source: SIE_STATUS.SETUP_REC

fields.name: SETUP_REQ
fields.description:
Set every time the device receives a SOF (Start of Frame) packet.
Cleared by reading SOF_RD

fields.name: DEV_SOF
fields.description:
Raised when any bit in ABORT_DONE is set. Clear by clearing all bits
in ABORT_DONE.

fields.name: ABORT_DONE
fields.description:
Raised when any bit in EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK is set. Clear by clearing
all bits in EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK.

fields.name: EP_STALL_NAK
fields.description: unspecified

fields.description: Host: Preable enable for LS device on FS hub

fields.name: PREAMBLE_EN
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_7_7
fields.description: Host: Delay packet(s) until after SOF

fields.name: SOF_SYNC
fields.description: Host: Enable SOF generation (for full speed bus)

fields.name: SOF_EN
fields.description: Host: Enable keep alive packet (for low speed bus)

fields.name: KEEP_ALIVE_EN
fields.description: Host: Enable VBUS

fields.name: VBUS_EN
fields.description:
After a write operation all bits in the field are cleared (set to
zero). Device: Remote wakeup. Device can initiate its own resume
after suspend.

fields.name: RESUME
fields.description:
After a write operation all bits in the field are cleared (set to
zero). Host: Reset bus

fields.name: RESET_BUS
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_14_14
fields.description: Host: Enable pull down resistors

fields.name: PULLDOWN_EN
fields.description: Device: Enable pull up resistor

fields.name: PULLUP_EN
fields.description: Device: Pull-up strength (0=1K2, 1=2k3)

fields.name: RPU_OPT
fields.description: Power down bus transceiver

fields.name: TRANSCEIVER_PD
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_19_23
fields.description: Direct control of DM

fields.name: DIRECT_DM
fields.description: Direct control of DP

fields.name: DIRECT_DP
fields.description: Direct bus drive enable

fields.name: DIRECT_EN
fields.description: Device: Set bit in EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK when EP0 sends a NAK

fields.name: EP0_INT_NAK
fields.description: Device: Set bit in BUFF_STATUS for every 2 buffers completed on EP0

fields.name: EP0_INT_2BUF
fields.description: Device: Set bit in BUFF_STATUS for every buffer completed on EP0

fields.name: EP0_INT_1BUF
fields.description: Device: EP0 single buffered = 0, double buffered = 1

fields.name: EP0_DOUBLE_BUF
fields.description: Device: Set bit in EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK when EP0 sends a STALL

record_types.name: SIE_STATUS_Register

fields.name: VBUS_DETECTED
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_1_1
fields.description: Read-only. USB bus line state

fields.name: LINE_STATE
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Bus in suspended state. Valid for device and host. Host
and device will go into suspend if neither Keep Alive / SOF frames
are enabled.

fields.name: SUSPENDED
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_5_7
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Host: device speed. Disconnected = 00, LS = 01, FS = 10

fields.name: SPEED
fields.description: Read-only. VBUS over current detected

fields.name: VBUS_OVER_CURR
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Host: Device has initiated a remote resume. Device:
host has initiated a resume.

fields.name: RESUME
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_12_15
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Device: connected

fields.name: CONNECTED
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Device: Setup packet received

fields.name: SETUP_REC
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Transaction complete.

 Raised by device if:

 * An
IN or OUT packet is sent with the `LAST_BUFF` bit set in the buffer
control register

 Raised by host if:

 * A setup packet is sent
when no data in or data out transaction follows * An IN packet is
received and the `LAST_BUFF` bit is set in the buffer control
register * An IN packet is received with zero length * An OUT packet
is sent and the `LAST_BUFF` bit is set

fields.name: TRANS_COMPLETE
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Device: bus reset received

fields.name: BUS_RESET
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_20_23
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. CRC Error. Raised by the Serial RX engine.

fields.name: CRC_ERROR
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Bit Stuff Error. Raised by the Serial RX engine.

fields.name: BIT_STUFF_ERROR
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. RX overflow is raised by the Serial RX engine if the
incoming data is too fast.

fields.name: RX_OVERFLOW
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. RX timeout is raised by both the host and device if an
ACK is not received in the maximum time specified by the USB spec.

fields.name: RX_TIMEOUT
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Host: NAK received

fields.name: NAK_REC
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Host: STALL received

fields.name: STALL_REC
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. ACK received. Raised by both host and device.

fields.name: ACK_REC
fields.description:
Write data bit of one shall clear (set to zero) the corresponding bit
in the field. Data Sequence Error.

 The device can raise a
sequence error in the following conditions:

 * A SETUP packet is
received followed by a DATA1 packet (data phase should always be
DATA0) * An OUT packet is received from the host but doesn't match
the data pid in the buffer control register read from DPSRAM

 The
host can raise a data sequence error in the following conditions:


* An IN packet from the device has the wrong data PID

fields.description: unspecified

fields.description: unspecified

fields.description: unspecified

fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: USBCTRL_REGS_Peripheral

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 1. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP1
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 2. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP2
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 3. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP3
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 4. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP4
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 5. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP5
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 6. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP6
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 7. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP7
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 8. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP8
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 9. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP9
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 10. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP10
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 11. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP11
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 12. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP12
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 13. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP13
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 14. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP14
fields.description: Interrupt endpoint 15. Only valid for HOST mode.

fields.name: ADDR_ENDP15
fields.description: Main control register

fields.name: MAIN_CTRL
fields.description:
Set the SOF (Start of Frame) frame number in the host controller. The
SOF packet is sent every 1ms and the host will increment the frame
number by 1 each time.

fields.name: SOF_WR
fields.description:
Read the last SOF (Start of Frame) frame number seen. In device mode
the last SOF received from the host. In host mode the last SOF sent
by the host.

fields.name: SOF_RD
fields.description: SIE control register

fields.name: SIE_CTRL
fields.description: SIE status register

fields.name: SIE_STATUS
fields.description: interrupt endpoint control register

fields.name: INT_EP_CTRL
fields.description:
Buffer status register. A bit set here indicates that a buffer has
completed on the endpoint (if the buffer interrupt is enabled). It is
possible for 2 buffers to be completed, so clearing the buffer status
bit may instantly re set it on the next clock cycle.

fields.name: BUFF_STATUS
fields.description:
Which of the double buffers should be handled. Only valid if using an
interrupt per buffer (i.e. not per 2 buffers). Not valid for host
interrupt endpoint polling because they are only single buffered.

fields.name: BUFF_CPU_SHOULD_HANDLE
fields.description:
Device only: Can be set to ignore the buffer control register for
this endpoint in case you would like to revoke a buffer. A NAK will
be sent for every access to the endpoint until this bit is cleared. A
corresponding bit in `EP_ABORT_DONE` is set when it is safe to modify
the buffer control register.

fields.name: EP_ABORT
fields.description:
Device only: Used in conjunction with `EP_ABORT`. Set once an
endpoint is idle so the programmer knows it is safe to modify the
buffer control register.

fields.name: EP_ABORT_DONE
fields.description:
Device: this bit must be set in conjunction with the `STALL` bit in
the buffer control register to send a STALL on EP0. The device
controller clears these bits when a SETUP packet is received because
the USB spec requires that a STALL condition is cleared when a SETUP
packet is received.

fields.name: EP_STALL_ARM
fields.description:
Used by the host controller. Sets the wait time in microseconds
before trying again if the device replies with a NAK.

fields.name: NAK_POLL
fields.description:
Device: bits are set when the `IRQ_ON_NAK` or `IRQ_ON_STALL` bits are
set. For EP0 this comes from `SIE_CTRL`. For all other endpoints it
comes from the endpoint control register.

fields.name: EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK
fields.description: Where to connect the USB controller. Should be to_phy by default.

fields.name: USB_MUXING
fields.description:
Overrides for the power signals in the event that the VBUS signals
are not hooked up to GPIO. Set the value of the override and then the
override enable to switch over to the override value.

fields.name: USB_PWR
fields.description:
This register allows for direct control of the USB phy. Use in
conjunction with usbphy_direct_override register to enable each
override bit.

fields.name: USBPHY_DIRECT
fields.description: Override enable for each control in usbphy_direct

fields.name: USBPHY_DIRECT_OVERRIDE
fields.description: Used to adjust trim values of USB phy pull down resistors.

fields.name: USBPHY_TRIM
fields.description: Raw Interrupts

fields.name: INTR
fields.description: Interrupt Enable

fields.name: INTE
fields.description: Interrupt Force

fields.name: INTF
fields.description: Interrupt status after masking & forcing

record_types.name: USBPHY_DIRECT_OVERRIDE_Register

fields.name: DP_PULLUP_HISEL_OVERRIDE_EN
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: USBPHY_DIRECT_Register

fields.name: DP_PULLUP_HISEL
fields.description: DP pull up enable

fields.name: DP_PULLUP_EN
fields.description: DP pull down enable

fields.name: DP_PULLDN_EN
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_3_3
fields.description:
Enable the second DM pull up resistor. 0 - Pull = Rpu2; 1 - Pull =
Rpu1 + Rpu2

fields.name: DM_PULLUP_HISEL
fields.description: DM pull up enable

fields.name: DM_PULLUP_EN
fields.description: DM pull down enable

fields.name: DM_PULLDN_EN
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_7_7
fields.description:
Output enable. If TX_DIFFMODE=1, OE for DPP/DPM diff pair. 0 -
DPP/DPM in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPP/DPM driving\n If TX_DIFFMODE=0, OE for
DPP only. 0 - DPP in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPP driving

fields.name: TX_DP_OE
fields.description:
Output enable. If TX_DIFFMODE=1, Ignored.\n If TX_DIFFMODE=0, OE for
DPM only. 0 - DPM in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPM driving

fields.name: TX_DM_OE
fields.description:
Output data. If TX_DIFFMODE=1, Drives DPP/DPM diff pair. TX_DP_OE=1
to enable drive. DPP=TX_DP, DPM=~TX_DP\n If TX_DIFFMODE=0, Drives DPP
only. TX_DP_OE=1 to enable drive. DPP=TX_DP

fields.name: TX_DP
fields.description:
Output data. TX_DIFFMODE=1, Ignored\n TX_DIFFMODE=0, Drives DPM only.
TX_DM_OE=1 to enable drive. DPM=TX_DM

fields.name: TX_DM
fields.description: RX power down override (if override enable is set). 1 = powered down.

fields.name: RX_PD
fields.description: TX power down override (if override enable is set). 1 = powered down.

fields.name: TX_PD
fields.description: TX_FSSLEW=0: Low speed slew rate\n TX_FSSLEW=1: Full speed slew rate

fields.name: TX_FSSLEW
fields.description:
TX_DIFFMODE=0: Single ended mode\n TX_DIFFMODE=1: Differential drive
mode (TX_DM, TX_DM_OE ignored)

fields.name: TX_DIFFMODE
fields.description: Read-only. Differential RX

fields.name: RX_DD
fields.description: Read-only. DPP pin state

fields.name: RX_DP
fields.description: Read-only. DPM pin state

fields.name: RX_DM
fields.description: Read-only. DP overcurrent

fields.name: DP_OVCN
fields.description: Read-only. DM overcurrent

fields.name: DM_OVCN
fields.description: Read-only. DP over voltage

fields.name: DP_OVV
fields.description: Read-only. DM over voltage

fields.name: DM_OVV
fields.description: unspecified

record_types.name: USBPHY_TRIM_Register

fields.name: DP_PULLDN_TRIM
fields.description: unspecified

fields.name: Reserved_5_7
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY\n DM pulldown resistor trim control\n
Experimental data suggests that the reset value will work, but this
register allows adjustment if required

fields.name: DM_PULLDN_TRIM
fields.description: unspecified

variables.name: USBCTRL_REGS_Periph
variables.code:
USBCTRL_REGS_Periph : aliased USBCTRL_REGS_Peripheral
  with Import, Address => USBCTRL_REGS_Base;
